name: arbor
description: Initialize Arbor state and run minimal framework checks for Arbor-created files plus project-level hooks; not a project-summary, health, maintenance, migration, or process-state report.

interface.shortDescription: Project memory plus explicit workflow skills
interface.longDescription: Arbor initializes project-local memory, registers executable project-level Codex hooks and runtime-specific hook wrappers, loads startup context in a stable order for fresh and resumed sessions, and runs minimal deterministic framework checks for Arbor-created or Arbor-managed surfaces only: AGENTS.md, .arbor/memory.md, CLAUDE.md, Codex project hooks, Claude project hooks, and packaged hook definitions. The arbor skill is detect-only by default and renders one strict table with Surface, Required, Status, Evidence, and Repair plus one Result line. It is not a project-summary, project-status, resume-summary, subjective health-advice, maintenance, migration, process-state, project-guide drift, docs/review, or feature-registry report. Project-level hooks are required for the selected runtime; packaged Claude hooks do not make missing Claude project hooks acceptable. Ordinary project overview answers should be direct and source-grounded after startup context is loaded, without selecting arbor unless initialization or an Arbor framework check is requested. Brainstorm turns scoped requests, bug reports without review context, planning continuations, and broad workflow changes into reviewable plans with acceptance criteria and test scope before routing approved work to converge. Feedback accepts feedback-shaped bug reports, regressions, failed checks, reviewer comments, and corrections to prior Arbor work when the next public owner is unclear, then routes only to brainstorm, converge, needs-evidence, or a direct response; it does not trigger from keywords alone or intercept another named public entrypoint that already fits. Converge is the public quality-loop orchestrator for existing managed features and drives the internal develop/evaluate cycle with release checkpoints before deciding whether the feature satisfies the plan. Develop, evaluate, and release are internal stages; they are not normal public user entrypoints, and users should not be asked to invoke release directly. Normal user-visible checkpoints render user_response and ui fields; raw workflow JSON, route labels, and terminal-state labels stay hidden unless the user explicitly asks for debug or machine output.
defaultPrompt: Run a minimal Arbor framework check for managed files and project hooks
Use brainstorm for planning, feedback for unclear feedback, or converge for current-loop repair
